response:
The old friendship notebook waits on the stair with your night tonight
The room keeps one pale lamp and one old light tonight
He folds the rent notice, then opens the drawer
The crack in the paint maps the years in patient sight tonight
He can still hear your laugh, though distance edits it thin
He hears it rise in the steam, then rise again on flight tonight
A tram rolls by and takes no message from this floor
He lets that pass, and keeps his breathing right tonight
The promise failed long ago and still returns on mornings
He still answers it with one white tonight

He counts the river stones and names each one by night tonight
The river replies in little pulses and steady light tonight
His foot slips where the water is glass and he steadies
The stones answer by their cooling, patient and clear in sight tonight
He waits until the first bus lights the bridge
He says the water can be wrong but never cruel on flight tonight
His son waits beside him and copies the ritual
She laughs at her own counting and calls it right tonight
By dusk each pebble is heavier than before
She leaves them all in his palm and leaves them bright tonight

We shoulder our bundles and map the way home by night tonight
We count every station by fear, and each warning light tonight
Our children ask which road leads to a name
We draw a circle in dust and promise that one reaches sight tonight
The platform steam lifts and smells of rust and tea
We stand in line, then step forward, still in flight tonight
You say this is not leaving, only a kind of becoming
I hold the handrail, then the railing holds me right tonight
The city folds us in its wind like paper
We do not turn back, but the past turns bright tonight

The market sweats in July and we cross it for your night tonight
You hold a folded umbrella above our heads and the light tonight
A man yells for ripe fruit beneath a crooked awning
The sugarcane cart rolls slow, then halts in sight tonight
Your shirt sticks to your shoulder but you keep the joke coming
I answer with a grin, though the air is hard as flight tonight
A stray dog sleeps beneath one crate and dreams in its own tempo
You kick gravel away from its paws and call it right tonight
At noon we buy two cold drinks and pay with small patience
Even heat remembers laughter and turns it white tonight

She returns to the river as dawn starts in night tonight
Her gloves hold smooth stones she once held in light tonight
She kneels and lets each pebble teach her a slower rhythm
The current takes the words and keeps their shape in sight tonight
She names the old names and lets the silence answer
Her breath draws slow lines across the cold and finds flight tonight
She waits until the first boathorn sounds at the bend
Then she stands, steadies her coat, and turns right tonight
By noon she has gone, but the stones remember her
She leaves no footprint, only the water bright tonight

You stand beneath the lamps after midnight, and the street is night tonight
You count each breath in the drizzle and answer it with light tonight
Your shoes click once at the curb then hold still
The glass reflects you whole but slightly blurred in sight tonight
A bakery shutter rattles and a cyclist drifts past
You do not move, you watch, you hold the hour in flight tonight
The wind turns your collar and you think of yesterday
You lift your chin and walk toward the stairs, right tonight
At the last light you pause and put your keys down once
You carry the whole dark home and leave it white tonight

He wades through the summer market in laughter and your night tonight
You tease the man at the melon stall and he answers with light tonight
The heat draws a shimmer above the road
A kite-shaped sign snaps and drifts near our sight tonight
I bargain for figs with your crooked grin
You answer the merchant, and both of us pay for flight tonight
Children run the aisles and steal only small air
They drop a paper hat and call it victory, right tonight
By the gate the sun burns the lane down to white dust
We carry the sound of it out into the dusk bright tonight

They meet on the park rail and test the old distance by night tonight
Your hand finds the same chipped paint where trust first went light tonight
The friendship is weathered, not broken all the way
It creaks but does not fail in patient sight tonight
You ask whether there is room for one more chapter
You ask it like a prayer, and I answer by nodding in flight tonight
A child asks for a sticker and the silence loosens
We give him two and give each other one right tonight
You stand and I stand and the bench keeps both our weight
The city keeps it all and keeps it bright tonight

You keep the atlas open and tell me this is return, not night tonight
You keep your map finger on the edge and keep the light tonight
The train leaves a long breath on every station glass
You read each destination and leave a small one in sight tonight
Your voice is steady even when your shoulders soften
You hold the station rail and your palm goes quiet on flight tonight
In your bag there are two pairs of keys, one new one old
You turn one, then turn the other, and call that right tonight
The train whistle cuts through the fog at the far track
You meet the old city again and still call it bright tonight

I cross the avenue where lamps gather under the night tonight
I keep my hands in my pockets and keep the light tonight
The apartment corridor hums like a tired radio
A neighbor's door closes and leaves one line in sight tonight
I drink too slowly and watch steam become rain
The kettle clicks in the dark, then pauses, then flight tonight
I hold the cup as if it can hold a decision
I do not drink it all, yet no part of me is right tonight
Outside the bus stop glitters with broken reflections
I turn around at the corner and keep that bright tonight

He sits by the wall where apartment songs escape at night tonight
He hears the trumpet climb and then soften in the light tonight
A child in the next room hums one bright, wandering note
The floor vibrates like a drum then fades in sight tonight
He taps the rhythm on the edge of his plate
The note slides down the hall and returns in flight tonight
He closes the window and loses nothing
He leaves the glass latched and opens the dark, right tonight
Morning finds him still where the music ended
He keeps that pulse with him, clear and bright tonight

The rain starts at dusk and stitches the road into night tonight
Your umbrella becomes a small moon and the light tonight
A cart wheel leaves a line by the corner store
The scent of mud lifts and rests in patient sight tonight
I watch the drops gather on the same old rail
They gather then break, then gather again on flight tonight
A neighbor calls out for sugar and I call back
No answer is needed, only one more right tonight
The lane quiets and the roof gives up its song
I stay a little longer, then leave by bright tonight

You stand under streetlights that count the night tonight
You map your steps with care and keep the light tonight
A cyclist passes, then vanishes behind a bus
The crossing stays red once longer than you expected in sight tonight
You stand where two roads disagree and choose the quieter one
You turn your watch face inward and let it spin on flight tonight
Your scarf lifts in wind and then settles again
You tuck it tight and keep your spine a little right tonight
There is no applause in this street, only the air
You walk away, and leave the whole night bright tonight

You keep your grief in a drawer where no key is needed at night tonight
You open it when the clock says morning and the light tonight
You touch old photographs with two fingers
The paper bends but refuses to lose the scent of sight tonight
A kettle sings once and then remembers its own quiet
You pour one cup and set it down in flight tonight
You do not name what hurts, you name the shape of your hands
You hold them still until the room agrees, and right tonight
The night turns toward white noise and gentle hum
You answer with breathing, then answer with bright tonight

We buy oranges by the basket and call this heat by night tonight
We bargain for figs and laugh at the same old light tonight
A cart wheel sticks on a seam of melted pitch
The fruit smells like honey and memory in sight tonight
You joke that the sun has moved into your pocket
I nod and shake out my hand, then hold the joke in flight tonight
We share one juice cup and no argument of blame
You call it thrift, I call it right tonight
By the tram stop the gulls have claimed the sky
We leave with our mouths wet and the air bright tonight

I sit by the rain at dusk and watch the glass keep my night tonight
I read your message once and keep the light tonight
A busker packs his instrument and slips a final note
The note stays in the corridor like a held note of sight tonight
I set one drop aside with my thumb
It trembles, falls, and vanishes with flight tonight
I write your name, erase it, then write it smaller
Then I keep the paper folded and the decision right tonight
The kettle clicks itself into silence
The room stays full and turns the rain bright tonight

You stand by winter branches, and fog draws its night tonight
Your breath makes small clouds against your own light tonight
The bare boughs scratch the window like cautious fingers
Their shadows cross the glass and sharpen in sight tonight
You turn your glove over and hold the seam with care
The old cold makes the seams sing then slip in flight tonight
You point at a sparrow and make it sound heroic
I laugh at the joke and the joke becomes right tonight
By midmorning the branches are still and bright as bone
You leave only warmth where your footprints were white tonight

You hold my hand and say we keep moving by night tonight
The road keeps changing names but keeps the same old light tonight
Our accent catches on the checkpoints and softens there
The station clock turns yellow when we begin to sight tonight
You keep the phrase of home folded like a passport page
I fold it in reverse and keep it with me for flight tonight
You joke with the porter and he nods through the fatigue
Then he lifts the bag and tells us what is right tonight
The train swallows the platform and does not look back
The window writes our faces in the dark, then bright tonight

We argue over peaches in the heat and call it evening night tonight
You steal one sweet fruit and call it courage in the light tonight
The vendor wipes his hands and gives us extra salt
The counter glass blurs our reflections into sight tonight
You say a day like this tastes like memory
I say memory has no recipe and nod in flight tonight
We leave with two loose notes and one shared umbrella
The street gives us a shortcut and we keep it right tonight
At the last corner we both keep our hands in pockets
The sunset turns the lane to paper and then white tonight

The train platform at dawn holds breath for its night tonight
The loudspeaker mispronounces our city twice in light tonight
A stranger with three suitcases smiles at my confusion
His smile says all departures share the same scent in sight tonight
I stand beside your bag while someone laughs into a phone
You pretend not to see and move one inch in flight tonight
The horn blows once, then once again
We board by number and step toward the day, right tonight
The window opens, closes, opens one final time
I keep your sleeve in my memory bright tonight

We are loud one hour and hollow the next after everyone leaves as night tonight
The house keeps the cups warm and the silence keeps the light tonight
Your coat is still on the back of the chair
Its sleeve catches the hallway air and trembles in sight tonight
I line the dishes without knowing why
One is chipped, one is clean, one has a line from flight tonight
The clock turns and keeps perfect time on the wall
I set it right, then sit, then stand, then hold that right tonight
At nine the wind slips under the door and moves the pages
I close both windows and let the dark turn bright tonight

You walk the street in rainlight and the lamps keep you in sight tonight
You keep to the same two blocks and the same small light tonight
The pavement is slick, and each puddle shows another you
You step, then step again, and count it out in right tonight
A siren passes then disappears behind the corner
Its echo follows like weather and drifts in flight tonight
You stand at your door and test the key twice
You open when it turns, and the hallway becomes right tonight
You put your shoes by the wall and breathe the small air
You switch off no lights, you switch on memory bright tonight

You stand in fog with winter branches and make the night tonight
The fog takes your edges and softens them in light tonight
The road ahead is white with distance and no hurry
One crow lifts from the curb and folds into sight tonight
You ask for my mitten and I give you mine
We trade for two breaths and then keep moving in flight tonight
You tell me to keep my gloves but no sermon needed
I answer with my hand at your elbow, right tonight
By morning the branchwork thins and the lane appears
We keep walking until each breath becomes bright tonight

You step into the house after everyone leaves and hear the night tonight
The hall lights one by one as if no one stayed for light tonight
Your shoe prints remain by the hall and then fade into dust
A curtain stirs once and then in tiny sight tonight
I open the kitchen and count every utensil
Nothing moved, then everything changed, then changes in flight tonight
You do not cry and do not explain
You set two cups, then one, then set it right tonight
The hallway narrows and widens with the weather
I leave the place as I found it, bright tonight

You face the salt wind on the gulf and keep your coat tight tonight
The waves throw their salt over stone and turn it into light tonight
Your words come thin and fierce, then settle
You ask the sea if it remembers us in sight tonight
A gull tears a scrap from the dock and lifts it up
It turns once, twice, then carries it on flight tonight
You laugh at your own old fear and call it good weather
You hand me a rope and tell me to pull, and pull it right tonight
The harbor keeps our shoes and gives them back clean
The night dries on the skin and becomes bright tonight
